_preset: context-management — a docs/knowledge repo where the point is keeping context true over time (no drift, one home per fact, fresh docs). Turns the CONTEXT rules up and skips build/test/service by declaring project_type=docs. Adjust the *_globs to your actual doc paths.
_status_file: CTX-01: the single owner file carrying a 'last-verified: <sha> <date>' stamp. Setting false (with a valid baseline.repo.json present) is an honored opt-out — orient replaces the status file.
_decision_globs: CTX-02/CTX-07: decision records must carry a Status; superseded ones link forward to a file that exists.
_doc_globs: CTX-05 (broken internal links) and CTX-10 (backticked paths resolve) scan these.
_sources_of_truth: CTX-03: declares where each kind of fact canonically lives, so there's one home per fact.
_grounding_docs: CTX-09 (opt-in): these docs must exist and be non-empty. List your load-bearing docs.
_freshness_globs: CTX-06 (opt-in): each listed doc must carry a 'last_review_date' in frontmatter, reviewed within doc_freshness_days. Add your long-lived docs.
_generated_globs: CTX-08 (opt-in): generated docs must carry a 'DO NOT EDIT' provenance marker so nobody hand-edits them. Adjust to where you generate docs.
_doc_lag_days: CTX-11: warns when a doc anchored to source (frontmatter 'sources: [glob]') lags that code by more than this many days.
_profiles: enables CTX-10 (code symbols/paths named in docs still resolve) — worth it for a context-focused repo.
